name: Anoxic mineral medium (per ref 16) with nitrate; ethane as sole carbon/energy source (PMC10811727)
description: Anoxic mineral medium (per ref 16) with nitrate; ethane as sole carbon/energy source — medium reported in PMC10811727 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC10811727 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Anoxic (NC10-type) mineral medium' (Recipe chains through two citations. PMC10811727 cites ref 16 = Wu M, et al. 'Anaerobic oxidation of propane coupled to nitrate reduction by a lineage within the class Symbiobacteriia' Nat Commun 2022;13:6115, DOI 10.1038/s41467-022-33872-y (PMC9576796). That paper defines its 'anoxic synthetic medium' by citing Ettwig KF, van Alen T, van de Pas-Schoonen KT, Jetten MSM, Strous M. 'Enrichment and M
provenance.verification_evidence: An anoxic mineral medium [16] of 0.67 and 1.69 l was initially added to the ethane and butane reactor ... Two anaerobic bioreactors seeded with activated sludge and anaerobic digestion sludge ... One was fed with ethane (C2H6) and nitrate ... resulting in the production of dinitrogen gas and ammonium.
provenance.recipe_evidence: PMC10811727 Methods: 'An anoxic mineral medium [16] of 0.67 and 1.69 l was initially added to the ethane and butane reactor ... The ethane/butane reactors were periodically flushed with pure ethane/butane gas (99.99%, Coregas, Australia) to maintain the ethane/butane partial pressure in the headspace between 0.9 and 1.2 atm. A concentrated stock solution (80 g NO3-N l-1) was manually pulse-fed to the reactors to replenish NO3- to 20-30 mg N l-1.' Ref 16 (Wu 2022) states the medium was 'anoxic synthetic medium prepared as described previously [45]' = Ettwig et al. 2009. Ettwig 2009 verbatim: 'T
provenance.base_medium_reference: Recipe chains through two citations. PMC10811727 cites ref 16 = Wu M, et al. 'Anaerobic oxidation of propane coupled to nitrate reduction by a lineage within the class Symbiobacteriia' Nat Commun 2022;13:6115, DOI 10.1038/s41467-022-33872-y (PMC9576796). That paper defines its 'anoxic synthetic medium' by citing Ettwig KF, van Alen T, van de Pas-Schoonen KT, Jetten MSM, Strous M. 'Enrichment and Molecular Detection of Denitrifying Methanotrophic Bacteria of the NC10 Phylum' Appl Environ Microbiol 2009;75(11):3656-3662, DOI 10.1128/AEM.00067-09 (PMC2687271), which gives the full composition.

components.name: carbon dioxide
components.role: inorganic carbon/gas phase (base medium sparged with Ar-CO2 95:5)

components.name: cobalt chloride
components.role: trace element (acidic TE stock 0.12 g/L CoCl2.6H2O)

components.name: copper sulfate
components.role: trace element (acidic TE stock 0.32 g/L CuSO4)

components.name: ferrous sulfate
components.role: trace element (acidic TE stock 2.085 g/L FeSO4.7H2O, dosed 0.5 mL/L)

components.name: manganese chloride
components.role: trace element (acidic TE stock 0.5 g/L MnCl2.4H2O)

components.name: sodium molybdate
components.role: trace element (alkaline TE stock 0.242 g/L Na2MoO4)

components.name: sodium nitrate
components.role: nitrogen source + electron acceptor (base 0.425 g/L = 5 mM; in this study additionally pulse-fed from an 80 g NO3-N/L stock to maintain 20-30 mg NO3-N/L in the reactor)

components.name: nickel chloride
components.role: trace element (acidic TE stock 0.095 g/L NiCl2.6H2O)

components.name: sodium nitrate
components.role: nitrogen source + electron acceptor (base 0.425 g/L = 5 mM; in this study additionally pulse-fed from an 80 g NO3-N/L stock to maintain 20-30 mg NO3-N/L in the reactor)
